name: Proyecto CubaEmprende
blurb: Archdiocese of Havana training program for private entrepreneurs (ProEmpleo model); a Cuba Study Group partner.

name: CubaEmprende Camagüey
blurb: Regional chapter extending the CubaEmprende training model to central Cuba.

name: AUGE
blurb: Private consulting and corporate-services firm advising 300+ Cuban private enterprises (founded 2014).

name: Merchise Startup Circle
blurb: Cuba's veteran developer and startup community, running meetups from Santa Clara to Havana.

name: InCuba
blurb: University of Havana innovation incubator (coordinated by Adriana Heredia).

name: Roots of Hope / Raíces de Esperanza
blurb: Miami nonprofit connecting young professionals to support Cuban peers; parent of StartUp Cuba.

name: StartUp Cuba
blurb: Media platform plus fellowships and workshops on Cuban entrepreneurship (launched 2014).

name: Cuba Study Group
blurb: Nonpartisan organization backing market reform and diaspora engagement; co-sponsor of Cuba Transformación and a CubaEmprende partner.

name: Cuba Emprende Foundation
blurb: Miami-based foundation supporting Cuban entrepreneurs and the CubaEmprende network.

name: Cuba Start-Up
blurb: How-to platform for starting and investing in Cuba (Yanoidis Mejias Mendoza).

name: Erich García Cruz
blurb: Founder & CEO of QvaPay — the Cuban fintech powering this Atlas — and of bitremesas; a leading builder of Cuba's diaspora payment rails.

name: Pricepeak AI
blurb: AI pricing venture founded by a CubaEmprende alumnus.

name: Cubazon
blurb: Diaspora-built commerce/logistics venture serving Cuban households.

name: Kewelta
blurb: Cuba-directed startup on the diaspora founder watchlist.

name: Knales
blurb: Cuba-directed startup on the diaspora founder watchlist.

name: Observatory on Cuban Economics (OCE)
blurb: Independent Cuban-economy research group; co-sponsor of the Cuba Transformación series.

name: Cuba Capacity Building Project / Horizonte Cubano
blurb: Columbia Law School program on Cuban legal and economic reform.

name: DRCLAS (Harvard)
blurb: Harvard's David Rockefeller Center for Latin American Studies — MSME/MIPYME research on Cuba.

name: FIU Cuban Research Institute
blurb: Florida International University institute studying Cuban and Cuban-American organizations.
